greeting.npc_text: Good morning. I'm afraid all visitors require a valid badge to enter the building.

choices.text: I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
What's wrong with the badge printer?
What happens if there's a fire?

greeting_return.npc_text: Still no badge printer paper, I'm afraid. Is there something else I can help you with?

choices.text: I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
I have a meeting inside. Surely I can just pop through?
What's wrong with the badge printer?
What happens if there's a fire?

no_badge.npc_text: No badge, no entry. Our printer is out of paper and IT hasn't responded in three days, so I cannot issue a temporary badge. I'm sorry.

tired_response.npc_text: I am absolutely shattered. Four hours at this desk, no break, no coffee, and I've explained the badge policy to seventeen different people. Each time slightly more slowly than the last.

coffee_preference.npc_text: Cappuccino. Every time. And here's the thing — that machine over there actually makes a proper one, if you know the trick. Nobody reads the manual, which is frankly criminal. Hold the Milk Froth button and tap Double Espresso twice. Hidden menu. Perfect cappuccino. I found it by accident while stress-pressing buttons during a particularly bad Monday. I'd get one myself but I cannot leave this desk or the entire security model collapses.

badge_printer_response.npc_text: Out of paper since Monday. I've logged a ticket, sent a follow-up, and sent a follow-up to the follow-up. At some point I stopped being sure if I was talking to a human or an auto-reply. The ficus has been more responsive.

emergency_response.npc_text: Fire protocol is very clear. All staff must escort visitors through to the designated assembly area — which happens to be inside the building, past this door. Why do you ask?

suspicious.npc_text: Right. Well. We haven't had a drill in three years and I'd very much like to keep it that way. Is there anything else I can help you with?
